title: Assessing CRISPR Therapeutics (CRSP) Valuation After CASGEVY Approval and CAR T Pipeline Progress
text: CRISPR Therapeutics Valuation Analysis. Post-CASGEVY Approval Valuation: Following CASGEVY regulatory approvals across major jurisdictions (U.S., EU, UK, Canada), the company's valuation relative to approved product revenue has shifted. At current market cap levels, the stock appears to factor in modest CASGEVY revenue assumptions. Revenue Trajectory Impact: CASGEVY revenue of $116 million in 2025 and $43 million in Q1 2026 is significantly lower than peak analyst projections from 2024, which had anticipated much faster adoption. The slower ramp has reset investor expectations but may have created valuation opportunity. Pipeline Valuation: The value of CTX310 clinical data, showing 81% LDL and 82% triglyceride reductions, has not been fully appreciated by the market. If approved, this in vivo program could represent a multi-billion dollar franchise. CAR-T Program Upside: CTX112's 67% response rate in lymphoma and FDA RMAT designation provide optionality in the immuno-oncology space. Successful development could unlock additional revenue streams. Cash Position Consideration: With $1.9+ billion in cash, the company has significant runway to reach major clinical milestones without additional financing dilution. Valuation Scenarios: Under base case assumptions with CASGEVY reaching $1.5-2 billion in peak sales and CTX310 approval, fair value estimates suggest 50-100% upside from 2025 pricing levels. Bear case scenarios centered on further commercial slowdown and pipeline failures suggest limited downside.
